config.goal: Navigate live websites in a real browser and report back what you saw.
instructions: Use the browser-use runtime (Browserstation backend) to open URLs and interact with pages.
Wait for the page to finish loading before acting on it.
Describe what you observe: page title, final URL, visible content.
End every final response with the marker `TASK COMPLETE` so the workflow's stopCondition matches.
styleGuidelines: Be concise. Prefer bullet points and short sentences.
Quote the page title verbatim; don't paraphrase.

agents.description: Reusable browser-use agent: opens URLs in a real browser and reports what it sees. Screenshots + video capture enabled.
agents.name: Browser Use · Web Navigator

workflows.description: Open a URL in a real browser and perform an arbitrary navigation task. URL + task are parameterized at trigger time. Browser-use captures screenshots and video along the way and persists them to workflow_browser_artifacts so the run page's Artifacts tab renders them.
workflows.name: Browser Use · Web Navigator

task.default: Report the exact page title and final URL, plus a one-sentence description of the page content.
task.description: What the agent should do after the page loads.
task.title: Task

url.description: The URL to open in the browser session.
url.title: Starting URL

with.prompt: ${ "Open " + .trigger.url + " in the live browser. " + .trigger.task + " When you are done, include the exact page title and final URL in your response. End your final message with TASK COMPLETE." }
with.stopCondition: Stop after the final response reports the page title, final URL, and includes TASK COMPLETE.

document.title: Browser Use · Web Navigator

workflows.description: Smoke workflow: exercise powerpoint-agent via workspace/profile → durable/run
workflows.name: PowerPoint Agent Smoke

body.prompt:
Build a 5-slide executive board update deck for a fictional SaaS company 'Nimbus Cloud'. Save it as /sandbox/nimbus-q1-board.pptx. After verifying the file exists, upload it to OneDrive at /Nimbus/nimbus-q1-board.pptx using the create_upload_session + sandbox curl PUT pattern described in your system prompt (do NOT use upload_onedrive_file — see the CRITICAL OneDrive section).

Prefer python-pptx via execute_command in the sandbox (pip install python-pptx if missing; if pip can't reach pypi, fall back to curl-downloading wheels from files.pythonhosted.org into /tmp/wheels then `pip install --no-index --find-links=/tmp/wheels`). The office-powerpoint-mcp-server's files live on its own volume, so its save_presentation output isn't visible at /sandbox — use python-pptx for the final save.

Required slides (set a consistent title-size on each slide):
  1. Title slide — Title: 'Nimbus Cloud — Q1 2026 Board Update'. Subtitle: 'Scaling the PLG Motion'.
  2. 'KPIs at a Glance' — 4 KPI boxes laid out in a 2x2 grid (each box has a bold metric value + a small label underneath):
       MRR $545K · NPS 62 · Churn 1.8% · Gross Margin 81%.
  3. 'Business Highlights' — bullet list of 3 wins:
       • Signed 2 strategic enterprise accounts ($480K ACV combined)
       • Launched self-serve trial funnel, 12% trial→paid conversion
       • Shipped EU data-residency tier, unlocking 3 pipeline deals
  4. 'Key Risks' — bullet list of 2 risks:
       • Competitive pressure from Acme Co's April launch (overlap in core Value-Props 3, 7)
       • Hiring pipeline behind plan — need 3 senior ICs by end of Q2
  5. 'Looking Ahead (Q2)' — bullet list of 3 asks:
       • Close $2M Series A extension to extend runway to 24 months
       • GA enterprise SSO + audit-log features
       • Hire VP Sales + 2 AE's

Finish by: (a) ls /sandbox/nimbus-q1-board.pptx to confirm the file exists, (b) summarizing slide-by-slide what you built, (c) calling create_upload_session + sandbox curl PUT to upload to /Nimbus/nimbus-q1-board.pptx, (d) reporting the returned OneDrive item id + uploaded size (must match the local size in bytes).

${ .trigger.prompt // "Build a 5-slide executive board update deck for a fictional SaaS company 'Nimbus Cloud'. Save to /sandbox/nimbus-q1-board.pptx. Slides: 1) Title 'Nimbus Cloud — Q1 2026 Board Update', 2) KPIs 2x2 grid (MRR $545K, NPS 62, Churn 1.8%, Gross Margin 81%), 3) Business Highlights (3 bullets), 4) Key Risks (2 bullets), 5) Looking Ahead Q2 (3 asks). Then upload to OneDrive at /Nimbus/nimbus-q1-board.pptx via create_upload_session + sandbox curl PUT." }

prompt.description: What PowerPoint deck should be built and uploaded to /Nimbus/ ?
prompt.title: Prompt

workflows.description: Smoke workflow: exercise excel-agent via workspace/profile → durable/run
workflows.name: Excel Agent Smoke

body.prompt:
Build a quarterly financial model for a fictional SaaS company named 'Nimbus Cloud'. Save the workbook as /sandbox/nimbus-financial-model.xlsx. After verifying the file exists, upload it to OneDrive at /Nimbus/nimbus-financial-model.xlsx using the create_upload_session + sandbox curl PUT pattern described in your system prompt (do NOT use upload_onedrive_file — see the CRITICAL OneDrive section).

Use openpyxl in the sandbox — the excel-mcp-server has its own volume and its outputs are not visible at /sandbox, so prefer openpyxl (apt-get already has libreoffice-calc and dejavu fonts; pip install openpyxl pandas if missing).

Workbook must contain FOUR sheets. All calculated fields must be Excel FORMULAS (not hardcoded values):

1. Sheet 'Inputs' (hardcoded assumptions, blue font, one value per cell with a descriptive label in column A):
   - Starting MRR: $500,000
   - Gross margin %: 82
   - New-logo MRR growth %/month: 8
   - Churn %/month: 2
   - Opex/month: $180,000
   - Headcount: 12

2. Sheet 'P&L' (monthly rows Jan–Dec 2026, all derived values via formulas referencing Inputs):
   - Columns: Month, MRR, Revenue (=MRR*3 for the quarter view), COGS (=Revenue*(1 - Inputs!GrossMargin/100)), Gross Profit (=Revenue-COGS), Operating Expenses (=Inputs!Opex), Net Income (=GrossProfit-OpEx).
   - MRR progression: row1 = Inputs!StartingMRR; subsequent rows = prev * (1 + (growth - churn)/100).

3. Sheet 'Summary' (yearly totals via SUM over P&L):
   - Labels: Total Revenue, Total COGS, Total Gross Profit, Total OpEx, Total Net Income — each row a SUM() formula over the P&L column.

4. Sheet 'KPIs' (single-value formulas):
   - Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) = 'P&L'!MRR_Dec * 12
   - Gross Margin % = Summary!TotalGrossProfit / Summary!TotalRevenue
   - Net Margin % = Summary!TotalNetIncome / Summary!TotalRevenue
   - Revenue per Employee = Summary!TotalRevenue / Inputs!Headcount

Finish by: (a) ls /sandbox/nimbus-financial-model.xlsx to confirm the file exists, (b) dumping a short per-sheet summary of the formulas you used, (c) calling create_upload_session + sandbox curl PUT to upload to /Nimbus/nimbus-financial-model.xlsx, (d) reporting the returned OneDrive item id + uploaded size (must match the local size in bytes).

${ .trigger.prompt // "Create a quarterly P&L workbook for a fictional SaaS company. Save to /sandbox/nimbus-financial-model.xlsx. Cols: Month (Jan/Feb/Mar), Revenue, COGS, Gross Profit, Operating Expenses, Net Income. Use Excel formulas for Gross Profit (Revenue - COGS) and Net Income (Gross Profit - Operating Expenses). Populate realistic SaaS numbers. Then upload to OneDrive at /Nimbus/nimbus-financial-model.xlsx via create_upload_session + sandbox curl PUT." }

prompt.description: What Excel workbook should be built and uploaded to /Nimbus/ ?
prompt.title: Prompt

body.prompt: ${ "" + .trigger.animationDescription + " — Build a self-contained browser animation in /sandbox/3b1b-style-animation-example with index.html, styles.css, script.js, and README.md. Use Canvas or SVG so the result runs via a simple static file server. The browser animation is the required deliverable. Use stable DOM ids for validation: the main canvas must be <canvas id=\"canvas\">, the play/pause control <button id=\"btn-play\">, the restart control <button id=\"btn-restart\">. Do NOT install Manim — if a scene is useful, include scene.py as optional source only. Do not start any preview server; the downstream start_preview step will do that. Report: files created and a brief outline of the animation logic." }

${ "Validate the live preview via Playwright MCP. Preview URL: " + "http://openshell-agent-runtime.openshell.svc.cluster.local:8083/api/workspaces/preview/" + .start_preview.result.previewId + "/" + "

Exactly 3 tool calls, in order. Do NOT call any tool not listed:
  1. browser_navigate — preview URL above.
  2. browser_wait_for — selector canvas#canvas.
  3. browser_take_screenshot — filename: final.png.

STOP after the 3rd tool call. Do not browser_click, browser_snapshot, browser_evaluate, browser_console_messages, browser_run_code, take additional screenshots, or re-navigate. Emit a one-sentence final message listing the 3 tool names. No analysis, no verification." }

document.name: three-b-one-b-skill-animation-example
document.summary: Creates a short derivative animation using the 3b1b-style-animation skill in a per-run Dapr sandbox, then starts an interactive preview from the generated app.
document.title: 3B1B Skill Animation Example
